instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Can Term Insurance with Disability Rider Insure You Against Disability?
Premature death is a definite financial loss, especially if the breadwinner of the family passes away. That is why there are life insurance policies that cover the risk of premature death and give a financial benefit in such contingencies.
While death causes financial loss, a disability is equally damaging. Though it does not claim life, disability can claim the ability to work. If that happens, your family will suffer a financial loss equal to that of your premature demise.
While term life insurance policies cover deaths, when it comes to disabilities, a disability rider is needed to secure against the financial implication of the contingency. That is why you can choose term insurance with disability rider for additional protection against disablements.
What is the accidental disability rider?
The accidental disability rider is an optional rider that comes with many life insurance plans, term plans included. The rider covers disabilities suffered due to an accident that occurred during the policy tenure. So, even if the insured does not die in an accident but becomes disabled, the rider would pay a financial benefit.
The rider covers permanent total and partial disabilities. Moreover, in many cases, a temporary total disability is also covered.
How does the rider work?
You can choose the accidental disability rider either when buying the term life insurance plan or at the time of renewal. You can also buy term insurance with accidental disability rider wherein the rider is inbuilt into the coverage.
Once you opt for the rider, it offers coverage throughout the policy tenure. In the case of accidental disablement during the tenure, the rider benefit is paid. This benefit depends on the severity of the disability. In the case of permanent total disablements, 100% of the rider sum assured is usually paid. However, in the case of permanent partial disability, a percentage of the sum assured is paid depending on the severity of the disability.
If temporary total disablement is also covered, then a percentage of the sum assured is paid on a weekly basis till you recover, subject to a maximum number of weeks as specified under the rider terms.
Types of disabilities covered under the rider
The disability rider covers specified disabilities that result from an accident. The disability should occur within a maximum of 180 days after an accident. The types of disabilities covered include the following –
Why choose term insurance with disability rider?
You can add the disability rider to your term insurance plan for a wider scope of coverage that covers both deaths and disablements. The benefits of term insurance with accidental disability rider include the following –
An additional financial benefit
The rider pays an additional financial benefit for a disability. Moreover, the benefit has flexible use. You can use it for your hospital bills, to pay off outstanding debt or to make the required modifications to your lifestyle. Thus, the rider helps you meet the different financial necessities that a disability can bring and gives you much-needed funds.
Secures your savings
By giving financial assistance during a disability, the rider helps you protect your existing savings and investments. The assets that you accumulate for your financial goals stay protected while the financial implications of the disability can be met with the rider.
Low-cost protection
The disability rider requires an additional premium payment. However, the premium is very low which makes the rider quite affordable. Thus, at a very reasonable premium, you can secure a high sum assured against disabilities and get low-cost protection.
The bottom line is:
Disabilities are unpredictable. They can cripple you financially and cause considerable financial strain. That is why, just like you secure your finances against premature death through term insurance, you need to make provisions for disabilities too. The disability rider allows you to do just that.
So, when you buy a term insurance plan, add the disability rider to the coverage. Alternatively, when you renew term insurance, you can choose the rider if not already added to the coverage. It would make your policy more comprehensive and ensure financial security in deaths as well as disabilities.
Note – KW – Term Rider – is not used as it was irrelevant in the context of the content.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. output.final_text:
How Can Term Insurance with Disability Rider Insure You Against Disability?
Premature death is a definite financial loss, especially if the breadwinner of the family passes away. That is why there are life insurance policies that cover the risk of premature death and give a financial benefit in such contingencies.
While death causes financial loss, a disability is equally damaging. Though it does not claim life, disability can claim the ability to work and earn. If that happens, your family will suffer a financial loss equal to that of your premature demise.
While term life insurance policies cover deaths, when it comes to disabilities, a disability rider is needed to secure against the financial implication of the contingency. That is why you can consider choosing term insurance with disability rider for additional protection against disablements on payment of nominal additional premium.
What is the accidental disability rider?
The accidental disability rider is an optional rider that comes with many life insurance plans, term plans included. The rider covers disabilities suffered due to an accident that occurred during the policy tenure. So, even if the insured does not die in an accident but becomes disabled, the rider would pay a financial benefit subject to terms & conditions of the rider.
The rider covers permanent total and partial disabilities. Moreover, in many cases, a temporary total disability is also covered.
How does the rider work?
You can choose the accidental disability rider either when buying the term life insurance plan or at the time of renewal. Once you opt for the rider, it offers coverage throughout the policy tenure. In the case of accidental disability during the policy tenure, the rider benefit is paid subject to terms & conditions of the rider.
The accidental disability rider covers specified disabilities that result from an accident and may vary from product to product. Generally, the disability should occur within a maximum of 180 days after an accident, for the rider benefit to be applicable.
Why choose term insurance with disability rider?
You can add the disability rider to your term insurance plan for a wider scope of coverage that covers both deaths and disablements on payment of nominal additional premium. The benefits of term insurance with accidental disability rider include the following –
1. An additional financial benefit
The rider pays an additional financial benefit for a covered disability occurred during the policy term. Moreover, the benefit has flexible use. You can use it for your hospital bills, to pay off outstanding debt or to make the required modifications to your lifestyle. Thus, the rider helps you meet the different financial necessities that a disability can bring and gives you much-needed funds. 2. Secures your savings
By giving financial assistance during a disability, the rider helps you protect your existing savings and investments. The assets that you accumulate for your financial goals stay protected while the financial implications of the disability can be met with the rider. 3. Low-cost protection
The disability rider requires an additional premium payment. However, the premium is affordable. Thus, at a reasonable premium, you can secure a sum assured against disabilities.
The bottom line is:
Disabilities are unpredictable. They can cripple you financially and cause considerable financial strain. That is why, just like you secure your finances against premature death through term insurance, you need to make provisions for disabilities too. The disability rider allows you to do just that.
So, when you buy a term insurance plan, consider adding the disability rider to the coverage. Alternatively, when you renew term insurance, you can choose the rider if not already added to the coverage. It would make your policy more comprehensive and ensure financial security in deaths as well as disabilities.
